title: Beautiful
text: This is truly a beautiful set.  The detail work is just what I imagined with each piece being Individual either with color or etching.  This is a very large set,  the board is 21 in  and the prices are perfect size, I love that the king and queen totally different and very detailed. The one thing that could be better is the peices need more weight, they seem a little light especially with how big they are.  I also think that if you know someone with a disability or poor eyesight that this set would be perfect. I also want to add that I received this in 3 days and I live in a rural area.  I added a few pictures because looking a real life pics helped me with my decision.

title: Great quality and look.  Easily the best set I've owned to date!
text:
NOTE: The images uploaded via "I. Steiner" are for the "Wegiel XL Royal Handcrafted Wooden Chess Maple Wood" (currently $79.99 here on Amazon) chess set, not the "Ambasador" (the name of this set, as it appears on the packaging, only has 1 "S") set featured on this page. So make sure you're looking at/purchasing the set you really want.

My "Ambasador" set arrived right on time, and in perfect condition. I knew the size was 20 x 20, but actually pulling it out of the box and checking out the pieces is quite impressive. It looks great, like something that may have been handed down by your father or grandfather. The colors are light enough, and with low enough contrast, that long periods of looking at the board won't strain the eyes.

I agree with some of the other reviews that it would have been nice for the pieces to be weighted a little bit, but I haven't had any issues during play. And one of the other reviews mentioned putting washers on the bottom of the pieces with an additional layer of felt, which I think would be easy enough to do if you really didn't like the weight of the pieces. Like I said though, it hasn't been an issue for me.

As you can see from one of my attached images, the playable area of the squares is almost exactly 2". So keep that in mind if you plan on buying any other pieces for other games (like Checkers pieces, etc...)

On a side note, I did purchased a set of the "Checkers Pieces in Black and Natural Wood - 1.5 in. diameter" here on Amazon. They are a perfect fit and look for this board.
_gate_reason: The review describes the chess set's colors, size, and overall impressive appearance.

title: Quality chess set.
text: It's a very nice looking set with large pieces.  The only issue I have is that the pieces are not weighted.  Good deal over all.
_gate_reason: Describes the chess set as nice looking with large pieces.

title: Unexpected Detail
text: I purchased this as a 5th anniversary present (wood) for my husband.  He was thrilled with the gift, and we were both pleasantly surprised by the quality and detail of the chess board, which appears to be hand-burned with decorative flourishes in addition to the very nice inlays for the squares.  It comes with letters and numbers inlaid as well, and really looks very lovely on the coffee table where it has been placed since it was received last month.  Furthermore, my three year old has played with it non-stop, and it has held up (pieces included, which I was worried about) remarkably well to the rough play of a young child.  I only have two small complaints about this product, and they are that the flimsy plastic insert that holds the chess pieces when the board is folded away is barely flocked and really doesn't feel like it will last a year without cracking and tearing, so I wish they had made it thicker and stronger and out of a nicer material.  The second regret is that the pieces themselves, while nicely carved and very detailed, are remarkably light and tend to skid around the board much easier than I would wish.  I knew they weren't weighted when I purchased the board, but I can't help regretting it now that I've actually played with it a bit.  Nevertheless, I think I would still make this purchase if I had it to do over again, and maybe someday I will invest in some of those outrageously expensive [[ASIN:B003T4G7MO Staunton, Weighted Tournament Chess Pieces w/ Wood Box]] to replace these.  I therefore tentatively recommend this very attractive product.
_gate_reason: The review describes the chess board's decorative details and inlays.

title: Great Quality With Warmth!
text:
I love it. It's probably not for tournaments but it's more for home. It looks and feels warm. The large size makes it feel more fun and relaxing. Please see the photos. In some photos the quarter looks more like a nickel but I used a quarter coin for a reference in all of the photos.

• BOARD - I was concerned how uneven and flimsy the board will be after reading a lot of feedback, but this is a really well made board. The top board piece might be about 1/8 inch thick. See the photos taken from both sides. One side doesn't fit as well as the other but it doesn't bother me. The back green pieces inside the board are not felt. They're made with flimsy plastic piece like the inside of a box for chocolate. The thickness is probably about that of the felt pieces on the bottom of the chess pieces.
• PIECES - I was curious how thick the bottom felt on each piece and how well they're attached to the shape of the bottoms. The pieces are not weighted but since they're large, they don't get knocked off easily so they don't bother me. They're all well made too.
_gate_reason: The review describes the board's thickness and materials, providing visual details.

title: Nice chess set. Would definitely recommend.
text:
The chess set I bought looked great "except one piece" in the set. That one WHITE KNIGHT was not carved well and looking ODD. When I approached the seller, I was told to contact manufacturer. I don't know how to do it for that piece.

Product: The board looked exactly the way it was described. The pieces looked great with its quality (except that one I mentioned above). It is definitely a great buy if you want to build more interest in playing chess.

Price: I did a lot of research around and nothing can beat this specific set for its price.

Rating: I rated my chess set as 3, because of that one BAD PIECE. I will update it further if manufacturer would send a replacement piece.

title: Buy it for the Board and Pair with Staunton #6 or Staunton #7 Pieces
text:
The folding board is delicate, but beautiful. Don't use it for speed chess. Close inspection reveals it to be a cheaply-made assembly-line product, but it sure looks fabulous. Hats off to the manufacturer for making a cheaply-made product look so good. The included pieces are too large for the board and require a board with 2.5-inch squares. Buy a set of Staunton #6 chess pieces from the same manufacturer, Wegiel in Poland, and you'll be happy. Staunton #7 pieces might be even better. I don't have a set of those.

I got this set for about $45 U.S., and at that price, it was a fabulous bargain. Beware of sellers trying to charge over $100 for this set. I've seen several.
_gate_reason: The review describes the board as delicate and beautiful, indicating its visual appeal.

title: Worth the gamble
text:
This was a gamble purchase. Frankly the price was hard to believe in the context of the few reviews that had been submitted. That said the pictures of the product seemed to back up the reviews so I took the gamble.

This is a hand carved wooden chess set which comes in its own box which doubles as a fold out chess board. The details on the pieces and board are exquisite, if you're looking for the hand-made look. By this I mean there are subtle differences in the carvings and markings of the "identical" pieces. To me this adds charm to the set. The chess pieces themselves are fairly large but unweighted. If you're looking for a championship weight chess set this is not it. However, if you have young ones who like to throw things this lack of weight is a distinct advantage. The case/board is laminated in dark and light wood to provide the board with a border of dark wood which is both carved and branded to provide decoration. The craftsmanship is excellent. The board top is light and would probably be damaged if it was hit with something heavy. It would not survive the attentions of a 3 year old armed with a mallet/Tonka toy/or any of the other items 3 years old tend to hit things with. That said if you can keep it away from rumbunctious children and are looking for an out of the ordinary, decorative chess set that screams "artisan made" and is highly playable this fits the bill nicely. We were so impressed by this set we purchased a second as a present for a family of chess players.
_gate_reason: The review describes the chess set's materials, details, and decorative features.

title: Very nice buy for the money
text:
The board and pieces are BEAUTIFUL. The set looks very rich and elegant. I am not disappointed when I pick it up to play. Yes, the pieces weight perhaps a little less than you might have hoped they would given how large and grand the whole set is, but do not get the false impression that these are pieces of paper, waiting to blow over in the wind! They are more than massive enough to stand their ground against clumsy hands rifling through the ranks and dealing out accidental bumps--you aren't going to have your game in disarray from falling, flying pieces. The pieces are made of a nice soft wood, beautiful in appearance, and the craftsmanship (no matter how they pulled it off) adds to the impression of a game that somebody actually cared about when they made it. Ditto for the wonderfully carved board. I was worried there'd be a seem where the joint of the case closes but no, it's a perfectly smooth join.

The only other con against the set I heard was a "glue smell". I have to agree, this is prominent when you first open the box, but it disappears quick, perhaps within one two two days, if you simply set up the board and pieces in the open. We suspect this is the glue used to adhere the form that holds the pieces when they are in storage. The guess being, when you build something en masse at the factory and quickly box it for shipping with volatile glues still inside, it's going to be an eye opening for whoever opens it later on. In our case, smell gone in days, never to return again.

We're enjoying the set immensely. I've finally come to grips with chess notation and am using it to learn from games of masters past, and my younger nieces and I are having a wonderful time playing head-to-head, *finally*, across a real chess board.
_gate_reason: The review describes the beautiful appearance and craftsmanship of the chess set.

title: Light but still worthwhile
text: I highly recommend this chess set. The board is very well crafted as are the pieces. As was noted in some of the other reviews, the pictures online give a good idea of what the product is like. I would draw attention to the size of the board; folded out, the board is almost 2 ft by 2 ft, so it is pretty big. However, even though it is large, the size of the board has not presented any problems with gameplay as of yet and has actually been pretty nice. Also, several of the other reviews note that the board is pretty thin. While this is true, the board is still not too flimsy. I do not think that the construction of the board need be of any concern. Another thing that was pointed out in some of the other reviews was that the chess pieces were not weighted. While this is true, it has not caused any problems. The chess pieces are solid wood and pretty large so they work fine for casual play (I would not recommend this set for tournament use). The only thing I do not like about this chess set is the inner lining for the chess pieces. As some of the other reviews noted, it is really thin. I would agree that if you are not careful you would probably brake the lining eventually. Even though the inner lining is a bit flimsy, I am still satisfied with my purchase. I think that as long as I am not too rough with the set it should last for a very long time. Overall, I would definitely recommend this chess set. It seems to be very well made (with the exception of the inner lining), and it looks great too.
_gate_reason: The review describes the size and materials of the chess set and board.

title: Pieces too big for the board
text:
Bought this board two years ago and have barely used it because the chess pieces are way oversize for the board.

My friend with whom I have been playing chess for several years insists on bringing his own board to my house, he says the pieces are so big they hide what's going on in the game. Today I finally ordered a new set of pieces, Staunton number 6, which is about as big as I see advertised, and they will be much smaller than the pieces that came with this board. Take a look at the picture with the pieces laying in the felt of the chess board, they are almost too big to allow the box to close.

Also, the squares are exactly 2" by 2", not 2.25 as shown in the product text.

There are websites that will tell you what size pieces are recommended for what board size. I recommend you look at those to make sure the board you buy matches the pieces
_gate_reason: Describes the size of the chess pieces and board squares.

title: Not satisfied for a $70 set
text: I just learned how to play chess and want to buy a high-quality one to play with my kids. I searched a lot here at Amazon and found this one with many good reviews, so I jumped in. It's big and the shape of the Queen, King and Bishop is very different so it's easy for beginners like us to tell and not to get confused, that may be the only good thing for this set, the only reason that I did not return it. However for a $70 set, I expect more, which is apparently missing in this set. The board is not made of solid wood but has cheap green plastic to hold the chessmen inside. As another reviewer had pointed out, the thickness of the board is just as thin as a piece of paper. Moreover, it gives strong smell of paint every time I took it out from the package box. On the package box, there are a few of typos in less than 100 English words. In summary, I will not recommend it to other people. I have experience with Amazon for 10 more years and this is my 1st product review, usually I am a little lazy to write a review, but since I depend more and more heavily on reviews from "real customers" to make a decision, I think it's better to say the truth here.
_gate_reason: Describes the board's material, shape, and thickness, indicating visual aspects.

title: You Know That Old Saying, "It Looks Good From My House"?
text: I want to suggest that you get what you pay for and I certainly do believe that I have gotten just that in this case. The set isn't bad, but it all depends on what you're looking for ( or the vantage point you are looking from): from afar, this set looks awesome, but once you get close up you see what I can only describe as inferior quality:  the basswood look and feel of the wood board; the crude "hand-made" details on the board; the cheap hinge and locks; the lightness of the pieces.  Considering all of this, you might feel a bit depressed; however, consider as well that you have acquired a very large chessboard with interesting pieces and you didn't spend scads of cash on it-- maybe you'll feel a little better if you do this. When I took the set out of the box and began examining it closely, I was ready to send it back; however, once I set it up and stepped away from it I was pleased by the purchase. If you really want that good feeling from close up, consider dropping a couple hundred more on a set.
_gate_reason: Describes the wood board's look, feel, and details.

title: First Rate for the Price
text: The look of the board and the pieces is Old World Craftsman.  It is pleasant just to see it set up in my game area.  There is just enough weight to each piece to give it a solid feel, or heft you might say.  The design and height of the Queen and King are sufficient to give the feel of a much more expensive set.  I like the idea of putting detail on each piece that is reminiscent of hand made individual craftmanship of old.  Most sets in this price range do not have such attention to detail.  The labeling of the squares is excellent for beginners and sometimes jogs the memory of past games for the more advanced player.  A great set for beginning youth, and can be used as they advance in age and skill. For travel, I think it leaves something to be desired.  The storage area for the pieces does not measure up the the rest of the set.  However, I leave it set up in my game area, I don't travel with this set, and it is very close to perfect for my purposes.
_gate_reason: Describes the board's Old World Craftsman look and detailed craftsmanship.

title: Ambassador Chess Set and Board
text:
The Ambaasador Chess Set and Board is an attractive hand crafted product combining high quality and ecological materials.The wood used is high quality plantation grown.

The board is wonderfully detailed with detail surrounding the algebraic notations and the pieces include distinctive kings and queens.

The pictures above do a good job of depicting the actual product.

The hinged board is of good size and quality with storage inside for the pieces although the surfaces are seemingly thin and therefore somewhat fragile. It seem's you could easily punch a hole in or the crack the board if you were'nt careful and inadvetently applied too much force to the surface.

Therefore I suggest this set for display or adults in an non-tournament environment.

The pieces are of a very good size and quality and are hand crafted although not weighted.

I purchased this set as a collectible for purposes of display and the occasional gameplay with my chess aficiondo friends.

I actually use a tournament grade set with triple weighted pieces purchased from USCF for actual gameplay.

At the time of my purchase, shipping was free.
_gate_reason: The review describes the attractive handcrafted quality and details of the chess set.

title: Jumbo, not grand
text:
There's a lot to like about this set. It's very large! But it has a certain rustic feel to it. The pieces are very light and have a very natural wood feel. Not polished at all, and no weight. You can smell the carpentry shop smell - which is a pleasant smell, but to me it doesn't smell like chess. The "jewels" in the queens' crowns are obviously glued on, and some in my set were a bit crooked. I bought it partly because it was large, and I didn't want some cheap, chinsy set. But the size of this set doesn't feel stately, it just feels "jumbo" - almost childish. I returned mine.

Don't get me wrong - the pieces are nicely carved, and other than the queens, there's no hint of sloppiness. It just didn't quite do it for me.
_gate_reason: Describes the set's size, rustic feel, and lightness of the pieces.

title: It's just ok.
text:
Mediocre workmanship and materials trying to be fancy.

From afar this board and pieces look nice, but upon handling and closer inspection, you find out real quick that for the $56, it's just ok. It's not a great value, but it's acceptable for that price.

Although some pieces have some jaggedness to their cuts, the pieces are the nicer part of the package in my opinion. They're on the lighter side, but I wouldn't label them junk or cheap.

The parts that make this seem more cheap than premium: felt lined tray to store pieces is made of very flimsy plastic, numbers/letters for grid look as if a child at summer camp made them while learning to wood burn, board is much lighter than you'd think, and there was about a 3/8" chip in the wood where the two halves meet together when unfolded.

If the letters/numbers were nicer and there wasn't that chip, I'd feel a lot better about the product. Definitely not something to give to an adult who's a serious player, but good for a kid or casual player.
_gate_reason: Review describes the board's appearance and quality of materials used.

I am not a huge fan of folk art but if you are this is easily and for sure the Chess Set of your wildest dreams.  This chess set is not meant to be ornimental it is meant to be used but not abused. title: Artisan level product for an affordable price - Aspergers approved!
text: My son and I bought this after looking through over a hundred different sets. I have a brother with Aspergers who loves chess but is oh so very particular about every aspect of his sets. The set had charm and quality and did not look like some cookie cutter board from a kids game store, this set is the REAL DEAL and my brother absolutely loves it, and we can tell without him saying it. title: Lovely, Awesome - Loving the crafting of the pieces. Green felt like storage slots is flimsy plastic which won't last long.
text: There seems to be many sizes of the same board and pieces and I made sure that I am ordering the 'Ambassador' model for the size of board and pieces I wanted. The Ambassador model comes with really good size chess pieces and the they are really well made, lovey to look. Kids love to play with his chess board. The board itself is very well crafted. Glad to buy this on sale for a very decent price. Chess lovers will enjoy this board and pieces. Pieces too are of a good weight. This item is impressive. The pictures make you feel that green felt to store the pieces are sturdy, but I was surprised that the green housing to store the pieces is a very thin plastic (sort of) material. Its flimsy as well and the item I received had the green felt  looking slots for the pieces broken in the corners. I didn't care to return as I feel that even if that breaks I will throw away that and use the folding board for storing the pieces. I must say that I liked the board and did choose to buy as it had nice slots for storing the pieces, which kids love to put back after they are done, but the broken plastic did not make me return the product (or exchange) as the important parts (board and pieces) are so lovely. title: Quality made in Poland, NOT China!!!
text:
Handmade and priced PERFECT!! Also made in Poland and NOT made in China!

I know $1,000+ boards exist, but this one has the look and feel of a very expensive board made at an outstanding price. Well worth every penny! It’s perfect for beginners and pro’s! I’ll put it this way, I never write reviews for any of the 1,000’s of items I’ve purchased through Amazon. This one was worth the time. I don’t know how many pictures a review holds, but I took about 10-15 photos I’ll try to upload here.

You won’t be disappointed! It is a quality board for the money. No cut corners. No cheap feel. No odors people are complaining about other than cut wood.

5 out of 5 Wegiel!

Thank you!! This will be in my family for generations to come.

title: Incredible handcrafted chess set, would buy another!
text:
This was the first time I'd heard of Wegiel, and after receiving and playing with this set a few times, my opinion is that it's well worth the money and I would absolutely buy another one. Handcrafted in Poland, this is a far cry from the many lower priced but low-quality sets found online. The chessboard/case we received lies perfectly flat & flush when opened, and the chess pieces are really incredible. Obviously not mass-produced by machine, but therein lies the charm.

Chess Piece Weight
I've seen many comments on how light these chess pieces are, and I was concerned before buying. However, after playing a few times, in my opinion it's not an issue at all. They are on the lighter side, but if I hadn't read the other reviews, I never would have noticed. It's certainly not worth the time & effort for me to rip off the felt bottoms & add weights. The pieces play fine, do not knock over too easily and are really a great size. Perfect for those with poor eyesight or big meathooks for hands - easy to handle & play with.

I wouldn't change a thing about this set. In my opinion, the negative comments I read are mostly nitpicking. For the quality of this Wegiel set, the fact that it's handmade in Europe, and the price, I have no reservations about giving it 5 stars.
_gate_reason: Describes the handcrafted quality and size of the chess pieces and board.

title: A really, really nice chess board.
text:
I wanted a a chess board that my kids could grow up playing on a "the family board". This is the size, heft, and hand-made quality that I wanted. The pieces feel substantial in your hand. The workmanship is wonderful. Personally, considering the details, I would say that it is worth 2-3x the price.

That is not to say there are no negatives, and here they are:

The board and pieces stunk when it was first opened. It was obviously from what was used to stain the wood and is understandable because any smell of the pieces was trapped in the box, but it did stink. I just left it in the garage for a few weeks before I brought it in. The smell doesn't stay forever, but it does need to air.

Also, there isn't enough clear distinction between the king and queen. The look is too similar for my taste and the height is too close. It's not a big deal once you get used to the look, but more of a height difference would have been welcome.

Bottom line:

The negatives don't change my rating. The first you can just air it out to not be a problem, and the second is a personal preference and small irritation. I have seen no other chess board of similar size and heft that gives you the feel that this is how the game was classically played.

Buy it, air it out first, and enjoy your games with a fine chess set.
_gate_reason: Describes the size, heft, and hand-made quality of the chess board.

If you want a high quality wood product, that's what the finish will smell like at first. The smell went away after a few days. The few touches that made it look handmade were beautiful. You could tell they hand burned the letters and numbers into each column. Added a very authentic/ craftsman touch to the board. The plastic insert with velvet lining where you store the game pieces is definitely on the cheap side, but not a big deal for such a high quality board and pieces. I will probably make my own foam insert at some point, but this casing will do just fine until then. Well worth every penny. The only thing I caution, the board and pieces are much bigger than we expected. Not a drawback at all for us, but for those looking for a regulation board, this one is much bigger than the standard size.  Weigel is definitely the brand to go with either way.

title: Quality
text:
The Ambassador Chess Set is a real value for the price. I paid almost double for just a set of chess pieces . I
like the Ambassador pieces much better. The Board is classic, looks elegent in any settings. I did add weight to all the pieces except the Pawns. The pieces are not weighted. It was quite simple to add the weight. I purchaesd a pack of 3/8' lead on Amazon, removed the felt from the bottoms, and drilled a 3/8' hole on the bottom of each piece. I drilled four for each for each King , three for the Queens and one deeper hole for each Bishop. I place the lead in the holes with a little glue and applied the new felt. The felt is also available on AMAZON. The circles comes in a pack with various sizes, the larger pads fit perfect. I found the best way to drill these pieces was to hold them tightely in your hand and drill the required depth. I started out making a Jig for my drill press, and found the hand held faster and less change of damaging the pieces.. I added from .5 oz to 2.5 oz to the various pieces. Major improvement.
I would recommend this Chess Set to my friends. There are many videos online explaining how to add the weight.
I was so pleased with this set, regardless of the weight issue, that I still rated it at 5 Stars.
_gate_reason: The board looks elegant and the pieces have specific weight modifications.

The handmade pieces are awesome, the fold-up board is awesome, the box it came in is awesome and the quality is also awesome for the price. The pieces are not weighted and they are lightish. They tend to slide if they are moved too fast (in other words not suited for blitz games), but they are handcrafted from solid wood and do have enough weight for a casual, untimed game at home. The board and pieces are huge and this set can be displayed in your living room with confidence. The craftsmanship is something to behold and catches the eye immediately.

It has to be said, though: the felt storage inside the box wasn't made to last, and I will probably have something custom made later on to replace it. As another reviewer pointed out, some of the sellers charge up to and above $100 for this product. I got this one from Amazon's own Continental Import & Export for about $55. They go fast, but I'd rather wait for Amazon to stock up than pay $100 or more.

I'm very happy with this set. As already said, the pieces are not weighted, but they sure are big enough to make up for it. You'd probably not use this set at your club or for tournaments, but it's perfect for home or a game in the park.

They're not super lightweight either, however. It actually almost feels as if some weight is implemented.

One minor complaint is the interior plastic-felt chessmen storage, which is prone to slip off from the board, as it's loose. It should be durable for a decent time, provided it is handled gently.

Despite the fact that the set came with a replaced black pawn from another set, resulting in a noticeably smaller pawn than the rest, I really have no other major complaints.

This set was well worth the price and makes a wonderful gift to any chess player. Makes me want to play chess every time I look at the set. Thus an overall 4.5/5 rating for the 4.5-inch King chess set sounds about right.

title: Beautifully crafted chess set
text:
I am super happy and pleased with this purchase. I bought this for my husband about 4 months ago as a graduation gift. I literally spent hours and days trying to find the perfect chess set- something a bit fancy and unique but also sturdy and well made. After reading a ton of reviews, I decided to purchase this one and I was not disappointed.

The chess board is beautiful and has such nice details. Once it's opened flat, it's almost seamless! It's so hard to find where the two edges are joined. I'm so happy that it is aligned so well.

The chest pieces are also so nicely made. Yes, they are light (in weight) but I don't think that bothers my husband at all.

If you're looking for a giftable chess set that's well made, this is a great option!

title: Big and beautiful (especially for the price!), but not a heavy duty luxury set.
text:
This is a beautiful chess set for the price.

The pieces look and feel hand carved. They are not too polished or overly refined, but they do not look poorly made either. The colors are rich and look good on the pieces. Unfortunately, some pieces have glue visible: for example, you can see the glue used to hold the balls on the top of the queens head.

They do look and feel large compared to many other chess sets I’ve used. However, they also seem light given their size. That said, they have plenty of weight for playing games around the house or even outdoors. The pieces might be just a little light for playing very fast paced games where players are moving very quickly and bumping pieces.

The board is a suitable size for the pieces and has a similar handmade look and feel. It also feels light and a bit thin for being made of wood. The hinges work well and so do the clasps, though they are very light weight and I wouldn’t trust them alone. If I take this board traveling, I’ll probably put a little tie or strap around it.

The green liner that holds the pieces inside the board is flimsy, but it seems to get the job done fine. This set came with only one set of queens. I wish I could order more from the seller.

Overall, I really love this set—especially at this price. If you want a smaller set or one that feels very polished and heavy, then this may not be for you. It’s too bad that there are no extra queens.
_gate_reason: The review describes the colors, size, and appearance of the chess pieces and board.

title: Fantastic Board
text: This chess set is one of my personnel favorites. It is very portable, the pieces are very strong, and it has a beautiful design. This chess set is very portable; you can easily travel with in a car. The chess set isn't too small or big, is it the perfect size. Also the pieces are very strong, I've dropped them a couple of time from high distances and they've never broke, and they are very big to easy to fit in your hand and they have beautiful carvings which show so much detail. The only problem is that underneath there's a little round patch to prevent the pieces to scratch the board, they do come off but they stick back on with glue. Furthermore on the board there's a beautiful design that takes you to another world, and the board has nicely sized and shaped squares and has all the numerations.
_gate_reason: The review describes the chess set's beautiful design and detailed carvings.
